preview_text:
The model rates Norway as the slight favorite, assigning them a 43% win probability against Senegal's 26%. This narrow margin reflects a competitive matchup on a neutral ground where either outcome remains credible, though Norway hold the statistical edge.

The draw carries meaningful weight at 31%, suggesting the contest is unlikely to be decided by a wide margin. The win probabilities are compressed across the three outcomes, indicating neither team commands dominant control of the match space. A close, potentially deadlocked affair is the model's central scenario.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices align closely with the model across all three outcomes, with gaps under 3 percentage points. Market and model broadly agree that Norway lean slightly favored while remaining vulnerable to either a draw or an upset, indicating convergence on the competitive tightness of this fixture.
